title: [$250] Odometer distance expenses to self-DM lose start/end readings

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
### Problem When you create an Odometer request to yourself, we don't show the start/end readings in the merchant field: **Expected:** **Actual:** When creating an odometer distance expense to an unreported/self-DM report, `odometerStart` and `odometerEnd` are not forwarded to the backend. The `requestMoney` function in `TrackExpense.ts` (called when `isUnreported=true`) never destructures or includes these fields in its `REQUEST_MONEY` API params, so the server stores no odometer NVPs and the merchant string omits the readings. ### Solution Add `odometerStart` and `odometerEnd` to the transactionParams forwarded through `requestMoney` in `TrackExpense.ts` (lines 1610–1850), or redirect unreported odometer distance expenses through the `createDistanceRequest` path (which already passes these fields correctly).
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @DylanDylann

  • Upwork Job ID: 2055436722541765277

Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to the workspace chat 3. Create a map distance request with a custom created distance rate (not the default distance rate) 4. Navigate to the workspace settings and delete the distance rate that was selected when creating the above expense 5. Go to Inbox ## Expected Result: After deleting the distance rate that was used to create the expense, an RBR should be displayed in the workspace chat, and the ‘Rate not valid for this workspace’ error should be displayed on the expense preview. ## Actual Result: After deleting a distance rate, the RBR in the workspace chat and the error on the expense preview appear only after opening the expense details. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24916d74-68d5-43e6-ba17-ce89cdf49852 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Web app crashes intermittently with no console errors

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Description The web app is crashing intermittently with no errors visible in the JS console after the crash occurs. This makes it difficult to debug since the console state is lost on crash. Most likely this crash https://expensify.sentry.io/issues/APP-2BN ## Steps to reproduce Unknown — needs reproduction. The reporter experienced multiple crashes on the web app but was not able to capture console output at the time of the crash. ## Expected behavior The web app should not crash unexpectedly. ## Actual behavior The web app crashes intermittently. No JS console errors are visible after the crash (likely because the page reloads and console is cleared). ## Additional context - Consider leveraging Sentry for better crash reporting so we don't rely on users having the console open - **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778782686789009
